description: Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, then by his son Pavel. Emerged during the war in Donbas (2014-2015), played a major role in the Russian invasion of Ukraine, and supported regimes in Syria, Libya, Central African Republic, and Mali. Accused of war crimes and linked to far-right extremism. Led an armed rebellion against Russia in June 2023.

country_links.evidence_quote: By March 2021, Wagner PMCs were reportedly also deployed in Zimbabwe, Angola, Guinea, Guinea Bissau, and possibly the Democratic Republic of Congo.
country_links.context: Wagner PMCs were reportedly deployed in Angola by March 2021.

country_links.evidence_quote: The organization was said to be registered in Argentina and has offices in Saint Petersburg and Hong Kong.
country_links.context: The organization was said to be registered in Argentina.

country_links.evidence_quote: Wagner mutineers would not be prosecuted if they chose to either sign contracts with the Defense Ministry or withdraw to Belarus.
country_links.context: Wagner mutineers were allowed to withdraw to Belarus after the 2023 rebellion.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Wagner Group had offices in 20 African countries, including Eswatini, Lesotho and Botswana, by the end of 2019.
country_links.context: Wagner Group had offices in Botswana by the end of 2019.

country_links.evidence_quote: Still, the Government of Ghana publicly alleged that Traoré began collaborating with the Wagner Group following the coup, enlisting the mercenaries against the jihadist rebels.
country_links.context: Wagner Group allegedly collaborated with the ruling junta in Burkina Faso, enlisting mercenaries against jihadist rebels and receiving a mine as payment.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Times reported that the Wagner Group flew in more than 400 contractors from the Central African Republic in mid- to late-January 2022 on a mission to assassinate Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy
country_links.context: Wagner Group has operated in the Central African Republic, flying contractors from there to Ukraine for a mission.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Sudanese Mining company, Meroe Gold, acting as a shell company for the Wagner Group, was able to use financial services provided by JP Morgan Chase to process a payment to a seller in China.
country_links.context: Wagner Group used a shell company to process a payment to a seller in China via JP Morgan Chase.

country_links.evidence_quote: By March 2021, Wagner PMCs were reportedly also deployed in Zimbabwe, Angola, Guinea, Guinea Bissau, and possibly the Democratic Republic of Congo.
country_links.context: Wagner PMCs were possibly deployed in the Democratic Republic of Congo by March 2021.

country_links.evidence_quote: In September 2024, it was claimed that PMCs from the Wagner Group (now known as “Africa Corps”) had entered Equatorial Guinea at the request of Teodoro Obiang.
country_links.context: Wagner PMCs entered Equatorial Guinea at the request of President Teodoro Obiang to help consolidate a succession of power.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Wagner Group is known to have operated in at least 11 countries; Russia, Belarus, Ukraine, Syria, Sudan, Mozambique, Central African Republic, Mali, Libya, Venezuela, and Madagascar
country_links.context: Wagner Group is known to have operated in Libya.

country_links.evidence_quote: Following the deployment of its contractors between 2017 and 2019, to Sudan, the Central African Republic, Madagascar, Libya and Mozambique, the Wagner Group had offices in 20 African countries...
country_links.context: Wagner Group deployed contractors to Madagascar.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 5 April 2022, Human Rights Watch published a report accusing Malian soldiers and Russian PMCs of executing around 300 civilians between 27 and 31 March, during a military operation in Moura...
country_links.context: Wagner mercenaries operated in Mali, executing civilians and suffering casualties in battles with Tuareg rebels and al-Qaeda affiliate JNIM.

country_links.evidence_quote: In August 2023, two Russian citizens were detained in Poland after they were spotted placing 'Wagner' stickers and other recruitment materials in public places.
country_links.context: Wagner Group conducted propaganda activities in Poland, with Russian citizens placing recruitment materials in public places.

country_links.evidence_quote: South Sudanese Civil War (military training and security)
country_links.context: Wagner Group provided military training and security during the South Sudanese Civil War.

country_links.evidence_quote: Following the deployment of its contractors between 2017 and 2019, to Sudan, the Central African Republic, Madagascar, Libya and Mozambique, the Wagner Group had offices in 20 African countries...
country_links.context: Wagner Group deployed contractors to Sudan and used a shell company there to process payments via JP Morgan Chase.

country_links.evidence_quote: Wagner has also supported regimes friendly with Russia, including in the civil wars in Syria, Libya, the Central African Republic, and Mali.
country_links.context: Wagner Group supported regimes friendly with Russia in the civil war in Syria.

country_links.evidence_quote: The group emerged during the war in Donbas, where it helped Russian separatist forces in Ukraine from 2014 to 2015. Wagner played a significant role in the later full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine, for which it recruited Russian prison inmates for frontline combat.
country_links.context: Wagner Group fought alongside Russian separatists in Ukraine from 2014-2015 and played a significant role in the full-scale invasion, recruiting Russian prisoners for combat.

country_links.context: Wagner Group is known to have operated in Venezuela.
